title: Air-fryer salmon
subtitle: Skin-on fillets, lemon, dill, eight minutes.
excerpt: Two salmon fillets, skin on, rubbed with olive oil, lemon zest, and a pinch of salt, cooked at 200°C for eight minutes in the air fryer until the centre is just opaque and the skin is crisp. Weeknight supper. Serves two.
sourceNotes: Air-fryer cooking is too recent for public-domain references; the timing here matches the consensus of major air-fryer manufacturer tests for 180 g salmon fillets. USDA / FDA guidance on cooked-fish internal temperature: 63°C for safety.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cooked salmon dries out after freezing.
recipe.batchNotes: Most domestic air fryers fit four fillets at a single layer. More than that, work in waves.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cooked salmon keeps cold in the fridge for two days; eat at room temperature in a salad rather than reheating.

content.text: Two skin-on salmon fillets brushed lightly with olive oil, seasoned with sea salt and a tight zest of lemon, slid into a pre-heated air-fryer drawer at 200°C and cooked for eight minutes until the flesh is just opaque to the centre and the skin underneath is mahogany and crisp. Eleven minutes start to finish; minimal washing-up; better fish than a grill gives.

The air-fryer's superpower with fish is the hot circulating air, which crisps the skin without overcooking the flesh. The salmon goes in cold from the fridge for the right gradient: cold inside, hot outside. Cooked at room temperature, the centre overshoots while you're trying to get the skin to crisp.

items.prepNote: half zested, half cut into wedges for serving

content.text: Set the air fryer to 200°C and let it heat for three minutes empty. A cold-start air-fryer underdoes the skin.

Pat the {{salmon-fillet}} of salmon fillets dry on both sides. Brush all over with {{olive-oil}} of olive oil. Season the flesh side with sea salt and a twist of pepper. Scatter the zest of half a {{lemon}} of lemon over the top.

Lay the fillets skin-side-down in the air-fryer drawer with a finger's width between them. Cook 8 minutes at 200°C. The flesh should be just opaque at the thickest point; if a knife flakes the centre cleanly, it's done. The skin will be crisp and mahogany.

Lift the fillets onto two warm plates. Rest 2 minutes; the centre finishes setting. Scatter with finely chopped {{dill}} of dill if using and serve with a wedge of lemon, a heap of green beans, and a spoonful of crème fraîche on the side.

items.cause: Fillets cooked from room temperature or oven set too high.
items.fix: Cold from the fridge, 200°C, 8 minutes for a 180 g fillet. Reduce time by 90 seconds for thinner fillets.

items.cause: Air fryer not pre-heated, or fillet skin still wet.
items.fix: Pre-heat 3 minutes. Pat the skin dry before the oil goes on.
Add a minute per extra 30 g of weight. Probe the centre with a knife if unsure.

items.cause: Drawer not oiled or the oil burned off in pre-heat.
items.fix: Brush the salmon, not the drawer. The fish releases when cooked through.

content.text: Air-fryer salmon is the weeknight midweek default — eleven minutes, three ingredients, fish that beats most pan-fried efforts. Eat with green beans and new potatoes in summer, or crushed peas and a baked sweet potato in winter.
